title: chionochloa rigida subsp. rigida
intro: Chionochloa rigida subsp. rigida, also known as rough tussock grass, is a type of perennial grass native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, skin conditions, and wounds, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and stems can be used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and salves.
